label: Google Spanner
description: Connect to Google Cloud Spanner, a globally-distributed, strongly consistent SQL database.

form.title: Connect Google Spanner
form.subtitle: Authenticate with a service account to query and manage your Spanner databases.

methods.label: Service Account JSON
methods.description: Paste a Google Cloud service account key JSON to authenticate. The simplest and most reliable path — no OAuth app setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Authenticate with a Service Account

This is the recommended approach for programmatic access to Cloud Spanner.

### 1. Create or select a service account

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and select your project.
2. Go to **IAM & Admin → Service Accounts**.
3. Click **Create service account** (or use an existing one).
4. Give it a name like `anton-spanner` and click **Create and continue**.

### 2. Grant the required role

5. Assign the role **Cloud Spanner Database User** (`roles/spanner.databaseUser`) — or **Cloud Spanner Viewer** if read-only access is sufficient.
6. Click **Done**.

### 3. Generate a JSON key

7. Click the service account you just created.
8. Go to the **Keys** tab → **Add key → Create new key → JSON**.
9. Download the `.json` file. Paste its entire contents into the **Service Account JSON** field below.

### 4. Find your instance and database IDs

10. In Cloud Console, go to **Spanner**.
11. Copy the **Instance ID** from the instance list (e.g. `my-instance`).
12. Click into the instance and copy the **Database ID** (e.g. `my-database`).

### Notes

- The JSON key is shown once. If you lose it, generate a new one and re-paste it here.
- Keep the project ID handy — it appears in the top-left project picker in Cloud Console.
- Rotate the key in Cloud Console under **Service Accounts → Keys** if you suspect exposure.

fields.name: service_account_json
fields.label: Service Account JSON
fields.description: Paste the full contents of the downloaded service account JSON key file.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: Project ID
fields.description: Your Google Cloud project ID — visible in the Cloud Console project picker.

fields.name: instance_id
fields.label: Instance ID
fields.description: The Spanner instance ID found in Cloud Console → Spanner → instance list.

fields.name: database_id
fields.label: Database ID
fields.description: The database name within the Spanner instance.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (User Credentials)
methods.description: Authenticate via a Google OAuth consent flow using your own Google Cloud OAuth client credentials. Suitable when you prefer user-level access instead of a service account.
methods.how_to:
> **Pick the OAuth client type that matches your Anton shell:**
>
> - **Desktop app (Anton.app on macOS / Windows)** → in *Application type*, pick **Desktop app**. Google auto-allows loopback redirects, so no redirect-URI registration is needed.
> - **Web (browser-hosted Anton)** → pick **Web application** instead, then under *Authorized redirect URIs* add `http://127.0.0.1:26866/v1/connectors/oauth/callback` (substitute your server origin if you're behind a reverse proxy or running on a non-default port).
>
> The walkthrough below was written for the desktop path. If you're on web, read every "Desktop app" reference as "Web application" and make sure the redirect URI above is registered before clicking **Submit**.

## OAuth 2.0 — Bring Your Own Client

This method uses Google's OAuth consent screen. You must supply your own OAuth client ID and secret from Google Cloud Console.

### 1. Enable the Spanner API

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and select your project.
2. Go to **APIs & Services → Library** and enable **Cloud Spanner API**.

### 2. Create an OAuth client

3. Go to **APIs & Services → Credentials → Create credentials → OAuth client ID**.
4. Set the application type to **Desktop app** and give it a name.
5. Copy the generated **Client ID** and **Client secret**.

### 3. Configure the consent screen

6. Go to **APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen** and complete the setup.
7. Add your Google account as a test user if the app is in testing mode.

### 4. Authorize in Anton

8. Paste the Client ID and Client secret below.
9. Fill in your Project ID, Instance ID, and Database ID.
10. Click **Submit**. Anton will open a browser window for you to grant access.

### Notes

- Refresh tokens are issued because `access_type=offline` is set.
- If you lose access, revoke at [myaccount.google.com/permissions](https://myaccount.google.com/permissions) and reconnect.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials. Use a Desktop app client.

fields.description: Paired with the Client ID from the same Credentials page.
